title: EVPN Ethernet Segment (ESI) DF Election and BUM Stability
spl:
index=network sourcetype="evpn:esi" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(event),"(?i)df|designated|esi|split|conflict")
| stats count by leaf, esi, event
| where count>5
| sort -count
| table leaf, esi, event, count
description: All-active multihoming depends on correct designated forwarder election and stable per-ESI state. DF flaps or split-brain indicators disrupt BUM handling and can isolate VLANs for dual-homed hosts. Early detection protects clustered databases and hypervisor trunks.
value: All-active multihoming depends on correct designated forwarder election and stable per-ESI state. DF flaps or split-brain indicators disrupt BUM handling and can isolate VLANs for dual-homed hosts. Early detection protects clustered databases and hypervisor trunks.
implementation: (1) Normalize DF change syslog into `evpn:esi`. (2) Alert on rapid DF changes per ESI within one hour. (3) Correlate with port-channel member events (UC-18.3.14).
visualization: Timeline (DF changes), Table (noisy ESIs), Single value (ESIs with recent DF churn).

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
ŌĆó Install and configure the required add-on or app: NX-OS/EOS syslog, custom `evpn:esi` parser.
ŌĆó Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=network` `sourcetype="evpn:esi"` with fields `leaf`, `esi`, `event`, `vlan`.
ŌĆó For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
(1) Normalize DF change syslog into `evpn:esi`. (2) Alert on rapid DF changes per ESI within one hour. (3) Correlate with port-channel member events (UC-18.3.14).

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype="evpn:esi" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(event),"(?i)df|designated|esi|split|conflict")
| stats count by leaf, esi, event
| where count>5
| sort -count
| table leaf, esi, event, count
```

Understanding this SPL

**EVPN Ethernet Segment (ESI) DF Election and BUM Stability** ŌĆö All-active multihoming depends on correct designated forwarder election and stable per-ESI state. DF flaps or split-brain indicators disrupt BUM handling and can isolate VLANs for dual-homed hosts. Early detection protects clustered databases and hypervisor trunks.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=network` `sourcetype="evpn:esi"` with fields `leaf`, `esi`, `event`, `vlan`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): NX-OS/EOS syslog, custom `evpn:esi` parser. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedŌĆörename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: evpn:esi. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

ŌĆó Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype="evpn:esi", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
ŌĆó Filters the current rows with `where match(lower(event),"(?i)df|designated|esi|split|conflict")` ŌĆö typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
ŌĆó `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by leaf, esi, event** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
ŌĆó Filters the current rows with `where count>5` ŌĆö typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
ŌĆó Orders rows with `sort` ŌĆö combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
ŌĆó Pipeline stage (see **EVPN Ethernet Segment (ESI) DF Election and BUM Stability**): table leaf, esi, event, count


Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (DF changes), Table (noisy ESIs), Single value (ESIs with recent DF churn).
grandmaExplanation: All-active multihoming depends on correct designated agent election and stable -ESI state ŌĆö so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
